macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stocks Rebound Sharply on Middle East De-escalation Hopes
items.summary: The S&P 500 rose 2.9% to 6,528.52, Nasdaq gained 3.8% to 21,590.63, and Dow added 2.5% to 46,341.51 amid improved risk sentiment from potential Middle East off-ramp and easing oil prices. Technology stocks led with Nvidia up 5.6% and Marvell surging 12.8% on AI optimism and investments.

items.title: Asian Markets Rally Following Wall Street Surge
items.summary: Japan’s Nikkei 225 jumped 5.0% to 53,621.08, Hong Kong’s Hang Seng rose 2.0% to 25,272.21, Shanghai gained 1.1% to 3,936.29, and Australia’s All Ordinaries up 2.3% to 8,885.60, lifted by global rebound and reduced geopolitical tensions.

items.title: Indian Sensex Surges Over 1,900 Points on Geopolitical Relief
items.summary: Sensex rose over 1,900 points and Nifty reclaimed 22,900 level, adding ₹13 lakh crore in market cap, driven by West Asia ceasefire hopes, positive global cues, and crude oil stabilization. Broad buying across sectors including banking, IT, and energy.

items.title: VIX Cools but Remains Elevated Amid Iran Risk and Oil Sensitivity
items.summary: Market volatility eased with VIX cooling after 1.3% S&P 500 move, though options show mild downside skew as investors hedge against lingering Middle East tensions and oil price fluctuations.

items.title: Global Markets Start April 1 with Constructive Tone Post-Rebound
items.summary: Financial markets opened positively on April 1, 2026, building on Tuesday's equity rebound as investors anticipate US manufacturing data and monitor geopolitical developments.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Shows Resilience as Bulls Stabilize Market
items.summary: April bitcoin futures prices are firmer in early U.S. trading, with bulls stabilizing the market this week despite bears maintaining technical advantage.

items.title: Russia Approves Crypto Regulation Bill With $3,300 Annual Limit for Retail Investors
items.summary: Russia has approved a cryptocurrency regulation bill that imposes a $3,300 annual transaction limit for retail investors, marking a significant regulatory development.

items.title: Ethereum and BNB Show Positive Price Movement
items.summary: Ethereum (ETH) gained 5.31% to $2,133.71, while Binance Coin (BNB) rose 1.53% to $614.64.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Arm Unveils 136-Core AGI CPU for AI Agents; Intel Executive Skeptical
items.summary: Arm announced a new 300-watt, 136-core CPU optimized for agentic AI workloads like OpenClaw, emphasizing performance, scale, and efficiency. Intel's Data Center Group leader questions its necessity, arguing existing CPUs suffice and highlighting sustainability issues with high-frequency boosts.

items.title: Anthropic Acknowledges Claude Code Users Exceeding Usage Limits Rapidly
items.summary: Anthropic reported that Claude Code, its AI-powered coding assistant, is hitting token usage quotas much faster than anticipated, frustrating Pro and Max plan subscribers who exhaust limits in hours. The company is prioritizing fixes amid user complaints on forums.

items.title: University of Manchester Develops ML Model for Ultra-Stable Molecular Simulations at 1000K
items.summary: Researchers created a physics-informed machine-learning model using Gaussian process regression that enables stable molecular simulations at extreme temperatures up to 1000 Kelvin, advancing drug development, materials science, and sustainable chemistry without supercomputers.

items.title: Alloy Therapeutics Launches AI-Powered Vigilance Division Led by Alexander Titus
items.summary: Alloy Therapeutics appointed Alexander Titus, Ph.D., to head its new Vigilance Division, which leverages AI to boost supply chain resilience, biosecurity, and therapeutic development for biological threats, collaborating with government and industry partners.

items.title: Global IME Bank Announces Second AI/ML Hackathon for Digital Banking Innovation
items.summary: Global IME Bank will host its second AI/ML Hackathon on May 29–31, 2026, targeting solutions like agentic fraud detection and multilingual voice banking, open to students, developers, and researchers with cash prizes and collaboration opportunities.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Epic Games to Cut More Than 1,000 Jobs as Fortnite Usage Falls
items.summary: Epic Games announced significant layoffs exceeding 1,000 employees amid declining Fortnite player engagement, marking a major shift in the gaming industry.

items.title: Neuralink Patient Confirms Playing World of Warcraft with Mind Control
items.summary: A Neuralink patient demonstrated the ability to play World of Warcraft using brain-computer interface technology, advancing neural gaming capabilities.

items.title: Pokémon Pokopia Launches IKEA Crossover in Japan
items.summary: Pokémon Pokopia began a crossover collaboration with IKEA on April 1, featuring Pokopia-themed areas and stamp rallies at select Japanese IKEA stores.

items.title: Newly Purchased Vizio TVs Require Walmart Accounts for Smart Features
items.summary: Vizio implemented a requirement for new TV owners to create Walmart accounts to access smart TV functionality, raising consumer concerns about account requirements.

items.title: Meta Loses Trial Over Child Exploitation Claims
items.summary: Meta lost a legal trial after arguing that child exploitation was 'inevitable' on its platforms, resulting in significant legal consequences.

items.title: Storage Prices Spiraling as CPU Shortage Intensifies
items.summary: Gaming PC manufacturers warn of escalating storage price increases and an intensifying CPU shortage, creating supply chain challenges for the industry.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Nvidia Invests $2 Billion in Marvell to Collaborate on Silicon Photonics
items.summary: Nvidia announced a $2 billion investment in Marvell as part of a partnership to develop silicon photonics technology. The deal deepens their collaboration amid ongoing tech market volatility.

items.title: Iran Threatens Attacks on US Big Tech Firms Starting April 1
items.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guard warned it will target major US tech companies like Google, Apple, Microsoft, and Tesla in West Asia from April 1 in retaliation for strikes on an Iranian pharmaceutical firm. The threat raises fears of cyberattacks and economic disruption.

items.title: Whoop Reaches $10.1 Billion Valuation After Latest Funding Round
items.summary: Wearables startup Whoop secured a new funding round that boosted its valuation to $10.1 billion. The CEO discussed the milestone amid a rally in tech stocks.

items.title: Field Materials Surpasses $1.3 Billion in Construction Purchasing Volume
items.summary: Field Materials hit $1.3 billion in volume driven by surging data center construction demand, with US spending projected to grow 23% in 2026.
